evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Extend governance of metric definitions and semantic layers; Implement monitoring, alerting, and anomaly detection systems
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (governance, metric consistency, testing, monitoring, anomaly detection) dominate with no velocity/shipping-speed language present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: Onboard new data sources like SAP and electricity costs into structured models
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role touches operational and cost data (SAP, electricity costs) affecting business decisions, but there is no financial-reporting, regulatory, or compliance language, keeping this at moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Design and maintain data models using dbt across business domains; Contribute to orchestration and data ingestion infrastructure
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools appear directly in responsibilities (dbt, Snowflake, Dagster) with an existing semantic layer and governance practice to extend, indicating a growing, mid-stage data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Onboard new data sources like SAP and electricity costs into structured models; Shape data infrastructure to support LLM-powered analytics tools
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name specific tools, data sources, and precise technical actions (dbt models, SAP onboarding, LLM-powered analytics infrastructure), letting the reader understand the actual day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Extend governance of metric definitions and semantic layers
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the data platform and its metric/semantic layer consumers rather than a single named commercial, product, or finance function.
evidence.autonomy_level: Extend governance of metric definitions and semantic layers; Onboard new data sources like SAP and electricity costs into structured models
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role combines strategic ownership of governance and semantic layer direction with scoped execution tasks like onboarding specific data sources, giving a mixed autonomy profile.
evidence.ai_role: Shape data infrastructure to support LLM-powered analytics tools
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build data infrastructure that downstream LLM-powered analytics tools consume, matching the ai_enabler pattern rather than using AI to accelerate their own coding.
evidence.testing_framing: The candidate should demonstrate commitment to "metric consistency and clear definitions" and emphasize data quality through testing and monitoring.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and data quality are framed as an owned commitment tied to governance responsibilities rather than a listed tool skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement monitoring, alerting, and anomaly detection systems
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Operational reliability concerns (monitoring, alerting, anomaly detection) are present and secondary to delivery, reflecting fear of pipeline/data failures rather than compliance or regulatory exposure.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Onboard new data sources like SAP and electricity costs into structured models; Contribute to orchestration and data ingestion infrastructure
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role both extends existing governance/models and onboards new data sources into structured models, giving a mixed greenfield/fix signal.
evidence.work_arrangement: Work Arrangement: Hybrid (2 days remote per week)
evidence.loss_aversion: "Implement monitoring, alerting, and anomaly detection systems" — operational reliability is a stated concern but secondary to delivery of data models and governance.
